mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value below 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 contradicting the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.5 percent threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate overheating consumer demand, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding corporate output and improving earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of recovery-phase observations, or the signal predictive power vanishes when tested outside recovery regimes with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income directly boosts household consumption and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth with Rank IC below 0.02 across a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in housing starts signals a collapsing construction sector and weakening consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when HOUST drops sharply, with 60-day forward returns positive in more than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting higher consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Moderate inflation combined with stable federal funds rates during a stable expansion regime supports corporate pricing power without triggering aggressive rate hikes, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns turn negative in more than 60 percent of stable expansion periods, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when inflation exceeds 4 percent.
